name: Floats
javadoc:
/** * Static utility methods pertaining to {@code float} primitives, that are not * already found in either {@link Float} or {@link Arrays}. * *

See the Guava User Guide article on * primitive utilities. * * @author Kevin Bourrillion * @since 1.0 */

members.name: hashCode
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Returns a hash code for {@code value}; equal to the result of invoking
 * {@code ((Float) value).hashCode()}.
 *
 * @param value a primitive {@code float} value
 * @return a hash code for the value
 */

tags.content: a primitive {@code float} value
tags.description: a primitive float value

tags.content: hash code for the value
tags.description: Returns a hash code for value; equal to the result of invoking ((Float) value).hashCode().

members.name: compare
members.javadoc:
/** * Compares the two specified {@code float} values using {@link * Float#compare(float, float)}. You may prefer to invoke that method * directly; this method exists only for consistency with the other utilities * in this package. * *

Note: this method simply delegates to the JDK method {@link * Float#compare}. It is provided for consistency with the other primitive * types, whose compare methods were not added to the JDK until JDK 7. * * @param a the first {@code float} to compare * @param b the second {@code float} to compare * @return the result of invoking {@link Float#compare(float, float)} */

tags.name: a
tags.content: the first {@code float} to compare
tags.description: the first float to compare

tags.name: b
tags.content: the second {@code float} to compare
tags.description: the second float to compare

tags.content: result of invoking {@link Float#compare(float, float)}
tags.description: the result of invoking Float#compare(float, float)

members.code:
/** * Compares the two specified {@code float} values using {@link * Float#compare(float, float)}. You may prefer to invoke that method * directly; this method exists only for consistency with the other utilities * in this package. * *

Note: this method simply delegates to the JDK method {@link * Float#compare}. It is provided for consistency with the other primitive * types, whose compare methods were not added to the JDK until JDK 7. * * @param a the first {@code float} to compare * @param b the second {@code float} to compare * @return the result of invoking {@link Float#compare(float, float)} */ public static int compare(float a, float b) { return Float.compare(a, b); }

members.name: isFinite
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Returns {@code true} if {@code value} represents a real number. This is
 * equivalent to, but not necessarily implemented as,
 * {@code !(Float.isInfinite(value) || Float.isNaN(value))}.
 *
 * @since 10.0
 */

 * @param target a primitive {@code float} value

 *     {@code -1} if no such index exists.
 */

members.name: indexOf
members.javadoc:
/** * Returns the start position of the first occurrence of the specified {@code * target} within {@code array}, or {@code -1} if there is no such occurrence. * *

More formally, returns the lowest index {@code i} such that {@code * java.util.Arrays.copyOfRange(array, i, i + target.length)} contains exactly * the same elements as {@code target}. * *

Note that this always returns {@code -1} when {@code target} contains * {@code NaN}. * * @param array the array to search for the sequence {@code target} * @param target the array to search for as a sub-sequence of {@code array} */

tags.name: array
tags.content: the array to search for the sequence {@code target}
tags.description: the array to search for the sequence target, not null

tags.name: target
tags.content: the array to search for as a sub-sequence of {@code array}
tags.description: the array to search for as a sub-sequence of array, not null

 * Returns the index of the last appearance of the value {@code target} in

 *     or {@code -1} if no such index exists.
 */

members.name: concat
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Returns the values from each provided array combined into a single array.
 * For example, {@code concat(new float[] {a, b}, new float[] {}, new
 * float[] {c}} returns the array {@code {a, b, c}}.
 *
 * @param arrays zero or more {@code float} arrays
 * @return a single array containing all the values from the source arrays, in
 *     order
 */

tags.name: arrays
tags.content: zero or more {@code float} arrays
tags.description: zero or more float arrays

tags.content: single array containing all the values from the source arrays, in order
tags.description: Returns the values from each provided array combined into a single array.

members.name: ensureCapacity
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Returns an array containing the same values as {@code array}, but
 * guaranteed to be of a specified minimum length. If {@code array} already
 * has a length of at least {@code minLength}, it is returned directly.
 * Otherwise, a new array of size {@code minLength + padding} is returned,
 * containing the values of {@code array}, and zeroes in the remaining places.
 *
 * @param array the source array
 * @param minLength the minimum length the returned array must guarantee
 * @param padding an extra amount to "grow" the array by if growth is
 *     necessary
 * @throws IllegalArgumentException if {@code minLength} or {@code padding} is
 *     negative
 * @return an array containing the values of {@code array}, with guaranteed
 *     minimum length {@code minLength}
 */

tags.name: array
tags.content: the source array
tags.description: the source array

tags.name: minLength
tags.content: the minimum length the returned array must guarantee
tags.description: the minimum length the returned array must guarantee, not negative

tags.name: padding
tags.content: an extra amount to "grow" the array by if growth is necessary
tags.description: an extra amount to 'grow' the array by if growth is necessary, not negative

tags.name: IllegalArgumentException
tags.content: if {@code minLength} or {@code padding} is negative
tags.description: if {@code minLength} or {@code padding} is negative

tags.content: array containing the values of {@code array}, with guaranteed minimum length {@code minLength}
tags.assertion: assert methodResultID.length >= args[1] && (methodResultID.length == args[0].length || methodResultID.fill(0, args[1]-args[0].length));
tags.description: an array containing the same values as array, but guaranteed to be of a specified minimum length.

members.name: join
members.javadoc:
/** * Returns a string containing the supplied {@code float} values, converted * to strings as specified by {@link Float#toString(float)}, and separated by * {@code separator}. For example, {@code join("-", 1.0f, 2.0f, 3.0f)} * returns the string {@code "1.0-2.0-3.0"}. * *

Note that {@link Float#toString(float)} formats {@code float} * differently in GWT. In the previous example, it returns the string {@code * "1-2-3"}. * * @param separator the text that should appear between consecutive values in * the resulting string (but not at the start or end) * @param array an array of {@code float} values, possibly empty */

tags.name: separator
tags.content: the text that should appear between consecutive values in the resulting string (but not at the start or end)
tags.description: the text that should appear between consecutive values in the resulting string (but not at the start or end), not null

tags.name: array
tags.content: an array of {@code float} values, possibly empty
tags.description: an array of float values, possibly empty, not null

members.name: lexicographicalComparator
members.javadoc:
/** * Returns a comparator that compares two {@code float} arrays * lexicographically. That is, it compares, using {@link * #compare(float, float)}), the first pair of values that follow any * common prefix, or when one array is a prefix of the other, treats the * shorter array as the lesser. For example, {@code [] < [1.0f] < [1.0f, 2.0f] * < [2.0f]}. * *

The returned comparator is inconsistent with {@link * Object#equals(Object)} (since arrays support only identity equality), but * it is consistent with {@link Arrays#equals(float[], float[])}. * * @see * Lexicographical order article at Wikipedia * @since 2.0 */

tags.name: <a
tags.content: href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lexicographical_order"> Lexicographical order article at Wikipedia

members.code:
/** * Returns a comparator that compares two {@code float} arrays * lexicographically. That is, it compares, using {@link * #compare(float, float)}), the first pair of values that follow any * common prefix, or when one array is a prefix of the other, treats the * shorter array as the lesser. For example, {@code [] < [1.0f] < [1.0f, 2.0f] * < [2.0f]}. * *

The returned comparator is inconsistent with {@link * Object#equals(Object)} (since arrays support only identity equality), but * it is consistent with {@link Arrays#equals(float[], float[])}. * * @see * Lexicographical order article at Wikipedia * @since 2.0 */ public static Comparator lexicographicalComparator() { return LexicographicalComparator.INSTANCE; }

members.name: toArray
members.javadoc:
/** * Returns an array containing each value of {@code collection}, converted to * a {@code float} value in the manner of {@link Number#floatValue}. * *

Elements are copied from the argument collection as if by {@code * collection.toArray()}. Calling this method is as thread-safe as calling * that method. * * @param collection a collection of {@code Number} instances * @return an array containing the same values as {@code collection}, in the * same order, converted to primitives * @throws NullPointerException if {@code collection} or any of its elements * is null * @since 1.0 (parameter was {@code Collection} before 12.0) */

tags.name: collection
tags.content: a collection of {@code Number} instances
tags.description: a collection of Number instances

tags.content: array containing the same values as {@code collection}, in the same order, converted to primitives
tags.description: Returns an array containing each value of collection, converted to a float value.

tags.name: NullPointerException
tags.content: if {@code collection} or any of its elements is null
tags.description: if {@code collection} or any of its elements is null

tags.content: (parameter was {@code Collection<Float>} before 12.0)

members.name: asList
members.javadoc:
/** * Returns a fixed-size list backed by the specified array, similar to {@link * Arrays#asList(Object[])}. The list supports {@link List#set(int, Object)}, * but any attempt to set a value to {@code null} will result in a {@link * NullPointerException}. * *

The returned list maintains the values, but not the identities, of * {@code Float} objects written to or read from it. For example, whether * {@code list.get(0) == list.get(0)} is true for the returned list is * unspecified. * *

The returned list may have unexpected behavior if it contains {@code * NaN}, or if {@code NaN} is used as a parameter to any of its methods. * * @param backingArray the array to back the list * @return a list view of the array */

tags.name: backingArray
tags.content: the array to back the list
tags.description: the array to back the list, not null

tags.content: list view of the array
tags.description: list view of the array

members.name: tryParse
members.javadoc:
/** * Parses the specified string as a single-precision floating point value. * The ASCII character {@code '-'} ('\u002D') is recognized * as the minus sign. * *

Unlike {@link Float#parseFloat(String)}, this method returns * {@code null} instead of throwing an exception if parsing fails. * Valid inputs are exactly those accepted by {@link Float#valueOf(String)}, * except that leading and trailing whitespace is not permitted. * *

This implementation is likely to be faster than {@code * Float.parseFloat} if many failures are expected. * * @param string the string representation of a {@code float} value * @return the floating point value represented by {@code string}, or * {@code null} if {@code string} has a length of zero or cannot be * parsed as a {@code float} value * @since 14.0 */

tags.name: string
tags.content: the string representation of a {@code float} value
tags.description: the string representation of a float value

tags.content: floating point value represented by {@code string}, or {@code null} if {@code string} has a length of zero or cannot be parsed as a {@code float} value
tags.description: the floating point value represented by string, or null if string has a length of zero or cannot be parsed as a float value
